reforms.description: The Polish government announced the creation of the Polish Investment Programme, which aims to co-finance investments in infrastructure and key industries using revenues from privatisation and government assets.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The programme is expected to enhance investment in key sectors, thereby fostering economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced by the government.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of a national investment programme represents a significant structural change in how investments are financed in Poland.
reforms.source_quote: In late 2012, the Prime Minister announced the creation of the Polish Investment Programme, which will use future privatisation revenues to co-finance investments in infrastructure.

reforms.description: Poland has implemented a broad deregulation of professional services, which is expected to increase competition in this sector by simplifying entry requirements and reducing unnecessary regulations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing competition, the reform is likely to enhance efficiency and innovation within the professional services sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been successfully implemented as of 2014.
reforms.importance_rationale: The broad deregulation represents a significant overhaul of the regulatory framework for professional services.
reforms.source_quote: Poland has recently implemented a broad deregulation of professional services, which should increase competition in this sector.
